title: mirabilis laevis subsp. glutinosa
intro: Mirabilis laevis subsp. glutinosa, also known as sticky wildey or sticky four o'clock, is a plant species in the four o'clock family that is native to California and Baja California. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Mirabilis laevis subsp. glutinosa include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for topical applications. The plant can be prepared as a salve or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat skin conditions and promote overall skin health.
